🌱 Bioregioning Earth

Bioregional Knowledge Commons

How to explore the Knowledge Commons

Organizations · Ontology · Sources · How to Explore the Knowledge Commons

This knowledge base is made of three interconnected sections. Here's what each one is for and how to move between them.

Organizations

Organizations is the best place to start. You'll land on a world map — each pin is an organization working in bioregional or regenerative practice somewhere in the world.

The map includes bioregionally oriented organizations, full case studies with detailed background and activities, and support organizations and learning networks that enable or connect bioregional work.

Click any pin on the map to open a side panel with the organization's details, or click the name in the table below to open its full page.

Ontology

Ontology is the shared vocabulary of the Knowledge Commons — the concepts and themes that cut across all the organizations. Think of it as a thematic index: if you're curious about a topic like governance, finance, or land stewardship, start here. Each concept links to the organizations and activities related to it.

There are two layers: themes and tags. The themes are broad topics that contain lots of different tags. You can see the full list of themes below. Click on the arrows on the left to uncover each theme's tags.

Sources

Sources is the bibliography — reports, essays, talks, and resources connected to the organizations and themes across this knowledge base. A good place to go deeper on something that caught your attention.

A few things worth knowing

  • This knowledge base runs on Notion, a tool that organises information in interconnected tables. You don't need to know how it works — just click what interests you and use the back arrow to return. If you ever feel lost, the links at the top of any page will bring you back to the main sections.
  • Not every organization has a full case study yet — the Knowledge Commons is a living project and some entries are more complete than others.
  • If you have suggestions, know of work that should be here, want to collaborate, or are just curious about what's behind it, reach out at yo@andreaisa.com.
Explore Organizations → ← Back to Dashboard
Bioregioning Earth — Powered by Notion + Astro
